title: Mac Miller 2009 lyrics — pain/cutting tab + five-people-rooming decision (4:51am Feb 26 2022)
html:
TL;DR

  • Single-line late-night note (4:51am, Feb 26 2022, freshman year at OSU) where Yahir captures that he had a tab open with 2009-era Mac Miller lyrics about "space, understanding suicide pain her cutting yourself" — alongside a second tab open about whether to room with five other people for sophomore year.
  • Two tabs, one moment: the lyric tab is processing pain (Mac Miller's death in 2018 at 26 was a cultural pain-marker for Yahir's cohort), and the housing tab is making a sophomore-year decision — the same five-roommate question that resurfaces in the March 16 calm checklist version.
  • The cuts off mid-sentence — "so I got" — never finished. Among the cleanest specimens in the vault of the late-night unfinished thought genre that defines a lot of Yahir's 2022 capture.
  • Captured during the freshman-spring stretch where housing decisions, identity questions, and music-as-emotional-substrate were all braided.
  • A year before the ChatGPT launch — this is pre-AI Yahir, just a 19-year-old at 4:51am with two tabs open.

Key fragment

"2009 Mac Miller lyrics space, understanding suicide pain her cutting yourself. Tab understanding if I should room with the five people tab, so I got"

Why it matters

Three-week pair to 2022 03 16 housing application questions — the same five-roommate decision shows up in two completely different states (4:51am pain-spiral; midday calm-checklist). This is the kind of paired-mood evidence that makes the vault honest about the texture of college-Yahir's interior life. Don't treat the calm note as the whole story.

Open questions

  • Which Mac Miller song specifically? (2009 era — K.I.D.S. mixtape, The High Life, The Jukebox.)
  • Does the "her" in the lyric capture refer to a Yahir-context girl or just to the lyric's narrator? (No way to tell from the note.)
